data.title: Over the past hour, more than $55 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network, with $23.09 million of that liquidation occurring in the Ethereum (ETH) market.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $55.834 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $52.6546 million in long positions and $3.1794 million in short positions. In addition, $23.09 million in ETH positions were liquidated and $18.39 million in BTC positions were liquidated.

data.title: Binance Wallet: Has blocked suspected malicious ZEROBASE website domains; related malicious contracts have been blacklisted.
data.description: Binance Wallet announced on its X platform that it has discovered a hacker attack on the ZEROBASE platform front-end, resulting in some users being tricked into authorizing malicious contracts. To ensure asset security, Binance Wallet has taken three emergency measures: blocking suspected malicious website domains, blacklisting related malicious contracts, and promising to send alerts to potentially affected users within 30 minutes.

data.title: ZEROBASE Issues Security Alert on Malicious Phishing Contract on BNB Chain
data.description:
According to ChainCatcher, the ZEROBASE team released a significant security announcement on December 12, 2025, warning users about a malicious phishing contract on the BNB Chain (BSC). This contract impersonates the official ZEROBASE interface, attempting to deceive users into granting USDT permissions.

The malicious contract is located at address 0x0dd28fd7d343401e46c1af33031b27aed2152396. In response to this threat, ZEROBASE has implemented a malicious authorization detection mechanism. If a user's wallet interacts with this contract, the system will automatically block deposit and withdrawal operations until the user revokes the authorization to the phishing contract.

ZEROBASE advises users to use revoke.cash or other tools to revoke suspicious authorizations and urges the community to remain vigilant. Users are encouraged to carefully verify all contract addresses displayed during wallet interactions and avoid using links from unofficial sources.

data.title: Security Alert Issued After ZEROBASE Front-End Hack
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a security warning has been issued by SlowMist founder Yu Jian following a hack on the ZEROBASE front-end. The attack led to some users inadvertently authorizing USDT to malicious contracts, resulting in asset theft. Users are advised to remain vigilant about their asset security.

data.title: Trump's eldest son followed Aster's partnership with USD1 on social media.
data.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of Donald Trump, shared and followed the collaboration between Aster and USD1 on the X platform. According to on-chain data, the RAVE/USD1 pair saw over $60 million in on-chain trading volume within three hours of its launch, with market activity continuing to heat up.

data.title: ZEROBASE front-end was faked and hackers stole over $250,000 USDT using phishing contracts.
data.description: According to a post on the X platform by Yu Xian, founder of SlowMist, and ZEROBASE, a malicious on-chain contract "Vault" (0x0dd2…2396) tricked users into authorizing USDT by impersonating the ZEROBASE frontend. This is suspected to be due to an attack on the ZEROBASE frontend, not a problem with the Binance Web3 wallet. Hundreds of addresses have been affected, with the largest single loss reaching $123,000. The stolen funds have been transferred to the ETH address 0x4a57…fc84. ZEROBASE has implemented an authorization detection mechanism, and the community is urging users to revoke the risky authorization as soon as possible via revoke.cash.

data.title: Coinbase Prepares Major Expansion With Prediction Markets And Tokenized Stocks
data.description:
Coinbase is set to unveil a significant platform expansion at a product showcase scheduled for December 17, 2025, according to a Bloomberg report. The company plans to announce the launch of prediction markets and tokenized equities, a move that underscores its ambition to evolve into a full-spectrum digital asset platform rather than a crypto-only exchange.

data.title: Binance responds to claims of "delayed response" or "insufficient action" regarding the Upbit hack: This is untrue.
data.description: Binance has responded to claims of a "delayed response" or "insufficient action" regarding the Upbit hack, stating that such claims are untrue. Binance's security and investigation teams discovered the incident immediately and took immediate steps to freeze related transactions and prevent further fund flows. Binance has maintained close cooperation with local law enforcement agencies and relevant partners, and continues to closely monitor the situation, providing support as needed. Any claims of "failure to take timely and effective action" are unfounded and inaccurate. Binance will continue to cooperate with relevant institutions and partners as appropriate.

data.title: Interactive Brokers Turns Stablecoins Into Instant Funding Rails For Traders
data.description:
Interactive Brokers has officially rolled out a new account funding option that allows clients to deposit funds instantly using stablecoins such as USDC (USD Coin) and USDT (Tether). The feature is powered by crypto infrastructure provider Zero Hash and operates around the clock, offering a faster and cheaper alternative to traditional banking transfers.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/interactive-brokers-turns-stablecoins-into-instant-funding-rails-for-traders/
